attackMethods.name: Phishing
attackMethods.description: Deceptive messages designed to trick users into revealing sensitive information.

attackMethods.name: Spear Phishing
attackMethods.description: Highly targeted phishing aimed at specific individuals or organizations.

attackMethods.name: Business Email Compromise (BEC)
attackMethods.description: Attackers impersonate executives to trick employees into transferring funds.

attackMethods.name: Password Spraying
attackMethods.description: Using common passwords across many accounts to avoid lockouts.

attackMethods.name: Brute Force Attack
attackMethods.description: Trying many passwords until the correct one is found.

attackMethods.name: SQL Injection
attackMethods.description: Injecting malicious SQL queries to manipulate databases.

attackMethods.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
attackMethods.description: Injecting malicious scripts into web pages viewed by others.

attackMethods.name: Session Hijacking
attackMethods.description: Stealing session tokens to impersonate users.

attackMethods.name: DNS Spoofing
attackMethods.description: Redirecting users to malicious websites by altering DNS responses.

attackMethods.name: Watering Hole Attack
attackMethods.description: Compromising websites frequently visited by a target group.

attackMethods.name: Drive-by Download
attackMethods.description: Malware automatically downloaded when visiting a compromised site.

attackMethods.name: Insider Threat
attackMethods.description: Employees or contractors abusing legitimate access.

attackMethods.name: Cloud Credential Theft
attackMethods.description: Stealing cloud access keys or tokens.

attackMethods.name: SSRF (Server-Side Request Forgery)
attackMethods.description: Forcing a server to make unauthorized internal requests.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware
malwareTypes.description: Encrypts files and demands payment.

malwareTypes.name: Trojan
malwareTypes.description: Malware disguised as legitimate software.

malwareTypes.name: Worm
malwareTypes.description: Self-replicating malware that spreads automatically.

malwareTypes.name: Rootkit
malwareTypes.description: Hides attacker presence and maintains privileged access.

malwareTypes.name: Spyware
malwareTypes.description: Collects user activity and sends it to attackers.

malwareTypes.name: Adware
malwareTypes.description: Displays unwanted ads and tracks browsing behavior.

malwareTypes.name: Fileless Malware
malwareTypes.description: Operates in memory without leaving files on disk.

malwareTypes.name: Keylogger
malwareTypes.description: Records keystrokes to steal credentials.

malwareTypes.name: Cryptojacker
malwareTypes.description: Uses system resources to mine cryptocurrency.

malwareTypes.name: Backdoor
malwareTypes.description: Provides unauthorized access to a system.

malwareTypes.name: Botnet
malwareTypes.description: Network of infected devices controlled by attackers.

malwareTypes.name: RAT (Remote Access Trojan)
malwareTypes.description: Allows attackers to remotely control an infected system.

malwareTypes.name: Logic Bomb
malwareTypes.description: Triggers malicious actions when specific conditions are met.

malwareTypes.name: Rogue Software
malwareTypes.description: Fake security software that scams users.

malwareTypes.name: Mobile Malware
malwareTypes.description: Malware targeting mobile devices.

malwareTypes.name: IoT Malware
malwareTypes.description: Malware targeting Internet of Things devices.

malwareTypes.name: Scareware
malwareTypes.description: Fake software that scares users into taking action.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS)
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware offered as a service to other criminals.

malwareTypes.name: Fileless Ransomware
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that operates in memory without leaving files on disk.

malwareTypes.name: Mobile Ransomware
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware targeting mobile devices.

malwareTypes.name: IoT Ransomware
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware targeting Internet of Things devices.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with Data Exfiltration
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that also steals data to increase leverage.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with DDoS Threat
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that threatens to launch DDoS attacks if ransom is not paid.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with Public Shaming
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that threatens to publicly release stolen data if ransom is not paid.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with Supply Chain Attack
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that spreads through compromised software updates or third-party vendors.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with Multi-Vector Attack
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that uses multiple attack vectors to increase chances of success.

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with RaaS and Multi-Vector Attack
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that combines RaaS with multiple attack vectors for maximum impact.
malwareTypes.defense: Comprehensive security strategy, EDR, backups, threat intelligence

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with RaaS, Multi-Vector Attack, and Data Exfiltration
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that combines RaaS, multiple attack vectors, and data exfiltration for maximum leverage.
malwareTypes.defense: Comprehensive security strategy, EDR, backups, threat intelligence, data loss prevention

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with RaaS, Multi-Vector Attack, Data Exfiltration, and DDoS Threat
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that combines RaaS, multiple attack vectors, data exfiltration, and DDoS threats for maximum impact and leverage.
malwareTypes.defense: Comprehensive security strategy, EDR, backups, threat intelligence, data loss prevention, DDoS protection

malwareTypes.name: Ransomware with RaaS, Multi-Vector Attack, Data Exfiltration, DDoS Threat, and Public Shaming
malwareTypes.description: Ransomware that combines RaaS, multiple attack vectors, data exfiltration, DDoS threats, and public shaming for maximum impact and leverage.
malwareTypes.defense: Comprehensive security strategy, EDR, backups, threat intelligence, data loss prevention, DDoS protection, public relations strategy

foundational.name: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
foundational.description: Adds an extra layer of authentication beyond passwords.

foundational.name: Least Privilege
foundational.description: Users only receive the minimum access required to perform their tasks.

foundational.name: Patch Management
foundational.description: Regularly updating systems to fix vulnerabilities and reduce attack surface.

foundational.name: Password Policies
foundational.description: Enforcing strong password creation and rotation requirements.

foundational.name: Network Segmentation
foundational.description: Separating networks to limit lateral movement.

detection.name: Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR)
detection.description: Monitors endpoint activity and detects suspicious behavior.

detection.name: Security Information & Event Management (SIEM)
detection.description: Aggregates and analyzes logs to detect threats.

detection.name: Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS)
detection.description: Monitors network traffic for malicious activity.

detection.name: Threat Intelligence Feeds
detection.description: Provides indicators of compromise and attacker behavior patterns.

detection.name: Behavioral Analytics
detection.description: Detects anomalies based on user and system behavior.

incidentResponse.name: Identification
incidentResponse.description: Detecting and confirming a security incident.

incidentResponse.name: Containment
incidentResponse.description: Isolating affected systems to prevent further damage.

incidentResponse.name: Recovery
incidentResponse.description: Restoring systems and returning to normal operations.

incidentResponse.name: Lessons Learned
incidentResponse.description: Reviewing the incident to improve future defenses.

red.name: Metasploit
red.description: Penetration testing framework for exploiting vulnerabilities.

red.name: Nmap
red.description: Network scanning tool used to discover hosts and services.

red.name: Burp Suite
red.description: Web application security testing suite.

red.name: Hydra
red.description: Password brute-force tool for multiple protocols.

red.name: BloodHound
red.description: Active Directory attack path mapping tool.

red.name: Cobalt Strike
red.description: Adversary simulation platform for red team operations and post-exploitation.

red.name: Sliver
red.description: Open-source command and control (C2) framework for red team engagements.

red.name: Empire
red.description: Post-exploitation framework with PowerShell and Python agents.

red.name: Mimikatz
red.description: Credential extraction tool for Windows environments.

red.name: Responder
red.description: LLMNR/NBT-NS poisoning tool for credential capture in internal networks.

red.name: Impacket
red.description: Collection of Python tools for working with network protocols and lateral movement.

red.name: CrackMapExec
red.description: Post-exploitation tool for lateral movement in Active Directory environments.

red.name: John the Ripper
red.description: Password cracking tool for offline hash attacks.

red.name: Hashcat
red.description: Advanced password recovery tool leveraging GPU acceleration.

red.name: Gobuster
red.description: Directory and DNS brute-forcing tool for reconnaissance.

red.name: FFUF
red.description: Fast web fuzzing tool for discovering hidden endpoints and parameters.

red.name: SQLmap
red.description: Automated SQL injection and database takeover tool.

red.name: Nikto
red.description: Web server vulnerability scanner for misconfigurations and outdated software.

red.name: Amass
red.description: Open-source reconnaissance tool for DNS enumeration and attack surface mapping.

red.name: Recon-ng
red.description: Web reconnaissance framework for open-source intelligence gathering.

glossary.definition: A security device or software that filters network traffic.
A security model that assumes no implicit trust and verifies everything.
Indicator of Compromise — evidence of a breach.
Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures used by attackers.
Regular outbound communication from malware to a command server.
Moving through a network after initial compromise.
Unauthorized transfer of data outside an organization.
Running code in an isolated environment to observe behavior.
Security Information and Event Management system.
Endpoint Detection and Response — monitors endpoint activity.
Intrusion Detection System — monitors for malicious activity.
Intrusion Prevention System — blocks malicious activity.
One-way transformation of data for integrity or authentication.
Converting data into unreadable form without a key.
Information about threats and attacker behavior.
Command and Control server used by attackers to manage malware.
Using one compromised system to attack others.
Combining multiple events to detect threats.
Reducing vulnerabilities by securing configurations.
All possible points where an attacker can enter a system.
Simulates attackers to test defenses.
Defends against attacks and protects assets.
Collaborates between red and blue teams for improved security.
Advanced Persistent Threat — a prolonged and targeted cyberattack.
A knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques.
Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures — a list of publicly disclosed vulnerabilities.
Common Vulnerability Scoring System — a framework for rating the severity of vulnerabilities.
Simulated cyberattack to test defenses.
Systematic review of security weaknesses.
Process of handling and mitigating security incidents.
Collecting and analyzing digital evidence after an incident.
Security Operations Center — centralized team responsible for monitoring and responding to security incidents.
Security Orchestration, Automation, and Response — automates incident response workflows.
Extended Detection and Response — unified threat detection across endpoints, network, and cloud.
Managed Detection and Response — outsourced security monitoring and incident response service.
Code or technique used to take advantage of a vulnerability.
A vulnerability unknown to the vendor with no available patch.
Compromising a trusted vendor or software dependency to attack a target organization.
Cloud Security Posture Management — identifies misconfigurations in cloud environments.
Cloud Access Security Broker — enforces security policies between users and cloud services.
Using stolen username and password combinations to gain unauthorized access.
Attempting multiple password combinations until the correct one is found.
Proactively searching for hidden threats within a network environment.
Documented step-by-step procedure for responding to security incidents.
Operational guide for executing technical processes or tasks.
Regular outbound communication from compromised systems to a command-and-control server.
Techniques used by attackers to move through a network after initial compromise.
Unauthorized transfer of data from an organization’s network.
Reducing vulnerabilities by securing configurations and removing unnecessary services.
Granular network segmentation to isolate workloads and reduce attack spread.
Collaboration between Red and Blue teams to improve detection and defense capabilities.
Exploits SMB vulnerabilities (EternalBlue) to encrypt files and demand ransom.
Targets enterprises and encrypts critical systems, often following initial access malware.
Uses double extortion by encrypting and exfiltrating data.
Ransomware-as-a-Service known for fast encryption and affiliate operations.
Operated by organized groups targeting enterprises with data theft and encryption.
Early ransomware that encrypts files and demands cryptocurrency payments.
Introduced data leak extortion alongside file encryption.
Encrypts the master boot record to block system access.
Destructive wiper disguised as ransomware targeting infrastructure.
Rust-based ransomware offering advanced customization.
Spreads via phishing and delivers additional malware payloads.
Steals credentials and enables lateral movement within networks.
Steals banking credentials using keylogging and form grabbing.
Spreads via malicious documents to steal banking information.
Used for credential harvesting and ransomware deployment.
Targets financial data using browser injection techniques.
Provides remote access and control of infected systems.
Captures keystrokes and steals credentials from infected machines.
Spreads through network vulnerabilities and weak credentials.
Email-based worm that creates large botnets.
Rapidly spreads by exploiting SQL Server vulnerabilities.
Targets industrial control systems and SCADA environments.
Advanced mobile spyware used for surveillance and data exfiltration.
Commercial surveillance spyware used by governments.
Targets executives via compromised hotel Wi-Fi networks.
Modular cyber espionage toolkit used for intelligence gathering.
Related to Stuxnet, used for reconnaissance and data theft.
Uses infected systems to mine cryptocurrency without consent.
Cryptomining worm exploiting SMB vulnerabilities.
Legitimate miner often abused for unauthorized Monero mining.
Unauthorized use of computing resources to mine cryptocurrency.
Malware that operates in memory without writing files to disk.
Attack technique that uses legitimate tools and processes for malicious purposes.
Compromising a trusted vendor or software dependency to attack a target organization.
An attack that exploits a previously unknown vulnerability with no available patch.
Distributed Denial of Service attack that overwhelms a target with traffic.
A business model where ransomware developers provide their malware to affiliates for a share of the profits.
An attack that uses multiple methods or vectors to compromise a target, such as combining phishing, malware, and DDoS.
The unauthorized transfer of data from a target to an attacker.

cloudSecurity.name: IAM Misconfigurations
cloudSecurity.description: Overly permissive roles or policies that allow privilege escalation.

cloudSecurity.name: Public Bucket Exposure
cloudSecurity.description: Cloud storage accidentally made publicly accessible.

cloudSecurity.name: SSRF in Cloud Metadata
cloudSecurity.description: Accessing cloud metadata endpoints via SSRF vulnerabilities.

cloudSecurity.name: Key Leakage
cloudSecurity.description: Cloud API keys exposed in code repositories or logs.

cloudSecurity.name: Misconfigured Security Groups
cloudSecurity.description: Overly open inbound/outbound rules exposing cloud workloads.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Ransomware
cloudSecurity.description: Ransomware targeting cloud workloads and data.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Account Takeover
cloudSecurity.description: Attackers gaining access to cloud accounts through credential theft.

cloudSecurity.name: Insecure APIs
cloudSecurity.description: Vulnerabilities in cloud service APIs that can be exploited.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Supply Chain Attacks
cloudSecurity.description: Compromising third-party cloud services to attack customers.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Data Exfiltration
cloudSecurity.description: Unauthorized transfer of data from cloud environments.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Misconfiguration
cloudSecurity.description: Incorrectly configured cloud resources leading to vulnerabilities.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Privilege Escalation
cloudSecurity.description: Exploiting vulnerabilities to gain higher privileges in cloud environments.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud DDoS Attacks
cloudSecurity.description: Distributed Denial of Service attacks targeting cloud services.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Insider Threats
cloudSecurity.description: Malicious or negligent insiders abusing cloud access.

cloudSecurity.name: Cloud Compliance Violations
cloudSecurity.description: Failing to meet regulatory requirements in cloud environments.

networkSecurity.name: Network Segmentation
networkSecurity.description: Separating networks to limit lateral movement.

networkSecurity.name: DNS Security
networkSecurity.description: Protecting DNS from spoofing, hijacking, and tampering.

networkSecurity.name: Zero Trust Networking
networkSecurity.description: Never trust, always verify — even inside the network.

networkSecurity.name: TLS Encryption
networkSecurity.description: Encrypting data in transit to prevent interception.

networkSecurity.name: Network Monitoring
networkSecurity.description: Inspecting traffic for anomalies and threats.

networkSecurity.name: VPNs and Remote Access
networkSecurity.description: Securing remote connections to the network.

networkSecurity.name: Intrusion Detection and Prevention
networkSecurity.description: Detecting and blocking malicious network activity.

networkSecurity.name: Network Access Control (NAC)
networkSecurity.description: Enforcing security policies on devices connecting to the network.

networkSecurity.name: DDoS Mitigation
networkSecurity.description: Defending against Distributed Denial of Service attacks.

networkSecurity.name: Network Forensics
networkSecurity.description: Analyzing network traffic to investigate incidents.

networkSecurity.name: Secure Network Architecture
networkSecurity.description: Designing networks with security in mind from the ground up.

networkSecurity.name: Wireless Security
networkSecurity.description: Securing Wi-Fi networks against unauthorized access and attacks.

networkSecurity.name: Network Segmentation
networkSecurity.description: Dividing a network into segments to improve security and performance.

networkSecurity.name: Network Access Control (NAC)
networkSecurity.description: Enforcing security policies on devices attempting to access the network.

networkSecurity.name: DDoS Protection
networkSecurity.description: Implementing measures to mitigate Distributed Denial of Service attacks.

networkSecurity.name: Network Forensics
networkSecurity.description: Analyzing network traffic to investigate security incidents.

networkSecurity.name: Secure Network Design
networkSecurity.description: Architecting networks with security principles in mind.

networkSecurity.name: Wireless Security
networkSecurity.description: Protecting Wi-Fi networks from unauthorized access and attacks.

networkSecurity.name: DNS Security
networkSecurity.description: Securing the Domain Name System against attacks like spoofing and hijacking.

networkSecurity.name: Zero Trust Networking
networkSecurity.description: Implementing a security model that requires strict identity verification for every person and device trying to access resources on a private network.

networkSecurity.name: TLS Encryption
networkSecurity.description: Using Transport Layer Security to encrypt data in transit and protect it from interception.

networkSecurity.name: Network Monitoring
networkSecurity.description: Continuously observing network traffic for signs of malicious activity or anomalies.

networkSecurity.name: VPNs and Remote Access
networkSecurity.description: Securing remote connections to the network using Virtual Private Networks.

networkSecurity.name: Intrusion Detection and Prevention Systems (IDS/IPS)
networkSecurity.description: Deploying systems that can detect and block malicious network traffic.

socOperations.name: Threat Hunting
socOperations.description: Proactively searching for threats without waiting for alerts.

socOperations.name: Log Analysis
socOperations.description: Reviewing logs to detect suspicious activity.

socOperations.name: Incident Response
socOperations.description: Coordinating actions to contain and remediate security incidents.

socOperations.name: Threat Intelligence Analysis
socOperations.description: Interpreting threat data to inform defenses.

socOperations.name: Security Orchestration and Automation
socOperations.description: Automating repetitive tasks to improve efficiency.

socOperations.name: Vulnerability Management
socOperations.description: Identifying and prioritizing vulnerabilities for remediation.

socOperations.name: SOC Metrics and Reporting
socOperations.description: Measuring SOC performance and communicating results.

socOperations.name: Collaboration with Red Team
socOperations.description: Working with red team to improve detection and response.

socOperations.name: Collaboration with IT and DevOps
socOperations.description: Working with other teams to implement security controls.

socOperations.name: Continuous Improvement
socOperations.description: Regularly reviewing and enhancing SOC processes and tools.

socOperations.name: SOC Shift Handover
socOperations.description: Ensuring smooth transition between SOC shifts.

socOperations.name: SOC Playbook Development
socOperations.description: Creating and maintaining playbooks for incident response.

socOperations.name: SOC Runbook Execution
socOperations.description: Following runbooks to perform specific tasks during incidents.

socOperations.name: SOC Training and Development
socOperations.description: Providing ongoing training to SOC analysts.

socOperations.name: SOC Tool Management
socOperations.description: Maintaining and optimizing SOC tools and technologies.

socOperations.name: SOC Incident Post-Mortem
socOperations.description: Conducting reviews after incidents to identify lessons learned.
